User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the BR TRIP application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

fullName.description: The full name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user.

FlightBooking.title: FlightBooking
FlightBooking.description: Represents a flight booking made by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the flight booking.

flightNumber.description: The flight number for this booking.

numberOfPassengers.description: The number of passengers for this booking.

bookingDate.description: The date and time the booking was made.

HotelBooking.title: HotelBooking
HotelBooking.description: Represents a hotel booking made by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the hotel booking.

city.description: The city where the hotel is located.

numberOfGuests.description: The number of guests for this booking.

bookingDate.description: The date and time the booking was made.

Itinerary.title: Itinerary
Itinerary.description: Represents a personalized trip itinerary generated for a user.

destination.description: The destination city or location for the itinerary.

startDate.description: The start date of the itinerary.

endDate.description: The end date of the itinerary.

description.description: A description of the itinerary, including activities and suggested travel plans.

createdDate.description: The date and time the itinerary was created.

DestinationGuide.title: DestinationGuide
DestinationGuide.description: Represents a destination guide with information on attractions, activities, and local customs.

id.description: Unique identifier for the destination guide.

description.description: A detailed description of the destination, including its history, culture, and attractions.

popularAttractions.description: A list of popular attractions in the destination.

localCustoms.description: Information on local customs and etiquette.

bestTimeToVisit.description: The best time to visit the destination.

structure.description: Stores user profiles. Uses path-based ownership for private user data.

definition.description: Stores flight bookings made by a user. Uses path-based ownership. Ensures only the user can access their bookings.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: flightBookingId
params.description: The unique identifier for the flight booking.

definition.description: Stores hotel bookings made by a user. Uses path-based ownership. Ensures only the user can access their bookings.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: hotelBookingId
params.description: The unique identifier for the hotel booking.

definition.description: Stores personalized trip itineraries generated for a user. Uses path-based ownership. Ensures only the user can access their itineraries.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: itineraryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the itinerary.

definition.description: Stores destination guides with information on attractions, activities, and local customs. Accessible to all users.

params.name: destinationGuideId
params.description: The unique identifier for the destination guide.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore data structure is designed to store user data, flight bookings, hotel bookings, itineraries, and destination guides for the BR TRIP application. The structure emphasizes path-based ownership for user-related data (users, flight bookings, hotel bookings, and itineraries) and a flat collection for destination guides, as these are likely global and shared across users. This approach facilitates straightforward security rules based on user IDs and avoids complex authorization logic. Each document is directly associated with a user, making it easy to manage and query data related to specific users. It also enables efficient data retrieval and management for personalized user experiences. Using path based ownership means the security rules will be simple. QAPs is supported because all list queries are scoped to `/users/{userId}`.
